---
pagetitle: "neuroim"
toc: false
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
# neuroim

[A typed Python surface for fMRI workflows. Silent space/mask mismatches become visible at the API surface, and derived-map provenance survives the file boundary in a public NIfTI extension.]{.lead}

[Quickstart](quickstart.md){.btn .btn-primary}
[Get started](get-started.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
[Receipt spec](spec/receipt-nifti-extension.md){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
[Browse the API](reference/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

## Why neuroim?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Contracts you can't forget

`series_roi`, `series_at_world`, `searchlight_apply`, `concat`, and `temporal_snr` validate the spatial frame at the API surface. A mask whose affine disagrees with the data raises *before* any numeric work; a world-coord outside the grid raises *before* a silent negative-index wrap. The failures these contracts catch are filed and runnable in [`examples/scenarios/`](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/tree/main/examples/scenarios).
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Provenance you can recover

Every typed result — `ROIExtractionResult`, `SearchlightResult`, `DenseNeuroVol.provenance` — carries a content-addressable `Receipt`: `method_name`, `input_space_hash`, `mask_hash`, `radius`, `n_voxels`, `source_affine_hash`. `to_nibabel()` embeds the JSON-serialized Receipt as a NIfTI `comment` extension (ecode 6, prefix `neuroim/receipt/v1:`); `read_image()` rehydrates it on the way back. The [extension is a public contract](spec/receipt-nifti-extension.md) — readable by **10 lines of `nibabel` alone**, no `neuroim` import required.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Evidence, not promises

Every claim above is anchored to a runnable scenario that compares the `neuroim` form against the raw `nibabel`+`numpy` form on the same task — line counts, contract coverage, and provenance round-trip behavior. See [`examples/scenarios/README.md`](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/README.md) for the full scoreboard.
:::
:::

:::

## In 30 seconds

A masked temporal-SNR map. The mask's spatial frame is checked at the API surface; the returned volume carries a `Receipt` whose hashes link back to the input space and mask.

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parent / "src"))

import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "docs" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

tsnr = bold.temporal_snr(mask=mask)    # raises if mask.space disagrees with bold.space
(tsnr.shape,
 tsnr.provenance.method_name,
 tsnr.provenance.input_space_hash[:12],
 tsnr.provenance.mask_hash[:12])
```

The Receipt survives the file boundary. After `ni.write_vol(tsnr, "tsnr.nii.gz")`, a fresh process calling `ni.read_image("tsnr.nii.gz")` returns a `DenseNeuroVol` with `.provenance` re-hydrated from the NIfTI extension — no sidecar JSON, no hand-curated manifest. The full pattern is in the [Quickstart](quickstart.md).

## Next

- [Quickstart](quickstart.md) — four canonical 5-line patterns, two side-by-side against raw `nibabel`.
- [Get started](get-started.qmd) — the same flow as a Quarto-rendered tour.
- [Receipt NIfTI extension spec](spec/receipt-nifti-extension.md) — the on-disk format for provenance, written as a public contract.
- [Scenarios scoreboard](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/README.md) — the runnable evidence behind every claim above.
